html:
[1]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 異聞總錄 - Title of the text, meaning 'Record of Strange Tales,' suggesting a collection of unusual or supernatural stories.
  • 大觀中 - Refers to the Da Guan era during the Song dynasty, a specific historical period.
  • 京師 - Refers to the capital city, specifically the Song dynasty capital of Bianjing (modern-day Kaifeng).
  • 醫家 - Refers to a family or household of doctors or medical practitioners.
  • 踰年 - Means 'for a year' or 'over the course of a year.'
  • 眷戀不忍去 - Means 'to be fondly attached and reluctant to leave.'
  • 祥 - In this context, it refers to the mourning period after the death of a close relative, specifically the year-long period of mourning.
  • 真厥妻也 - Means 'she is truly his wife,' with '厥' referring to the man mentioned earlier.
  • 佯為 - Means 'pretend to be' or 'feign to be.'
  • 徘徊道上 - Means 'to loiter or wander on the road.'
  • 伺其出而見之 - Means 'to wait for her to come out and see her.'
  • 語人曰 - Means 'to tell people' or 'to say to others.'
  • 迫于饥馁 - Means 'forced by hunger and starvation.'
  • 自鬻 - Means 'to sell oneself' or 'to prostitute oneself.'
  • 迹所从来 - Means 'to trace the origin' or 'to investigate the source.'
  • 昏然不省忆 - Means 'to be confused and unable to recall.'
  • 命夫以余直偿耿氏而取其妻 - Means 'to order the husband to pay the remaining price to the seller and take his wife.'
  • 不一年 - Means 'not a year' or 'within a year.'
  • 家亦衰替 - Means 'the family also declined.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 大觀 - The era name of Emperor Huizong of Song (1107–1110), indicating the historical context of the story.
  • 京師 - The capital city of the Song dynasty, Bianjing (modern-day Kaifeng), which was a major political and cultural center.
  • 醫家 - A family or household of doctors, reflecting the social structure of the time where medical knowledge was often passed down through families.
  • 祥 - The year-long mourning period after the death of a close relative, a significant cultural practice in ancient China.
  • 广备桥 - A specific location in Bianjing, providing geographical context to the story.
  • 牙媪 - A middle-aged woman who acts as a matchmaker or broker, a common figure in ancient Chinese society.

Proper Nouns

  • 大觀 - The era name of Emperor Huizong of Song.
  • 京師 - The capital city of the Song dynasty, Bianjing (modern-day Kaifeng).
  • 耿愚 - The name of the doctor who bought the servant girl.
  • 廣備橋 - A specific bridge in Bianjing.
  • 牙媪 - A matchmaker or broker.

[2]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 宣和中 - Refers to the Xuanhe period (1119-1125) of the Northern Song dynasty.
  • 鄉人 - Means 'villager' or 'native of the village.'
  • 州學 - Refers to the prefectural school, a local government-sponsored educational institution.
  • 如厕 - Literally 'to the toilet,' used here to mean 'going to the restroom.'
  • 白衣婦人 - Literally 'white-clad woman,' used to describe a ghost or spirit.
  • 徘徊於前 - Means 'lingered in front of.'
  • 良人已末没 - '良人' refers to a husband, and '末没' means 'not yet dead,' so the phrase means 'my husband has not yet died.'
  • 藐然无所归 - '藐然' means 'pitifully' or 'helplessly,' and '无所归' means 'without a place to return to,' so the phrase means 'pitifully without a place to return to.'
  • 留与语 - Means 'spoke with her.'
  • 斋舍 - Refers to a place of study or a dormitory.
  • 并寝 - Means 'slept together.'
  • 得疾 - Means 'fell ill.'
  • 同舍生 - Refers to fellow students in the same dormitory.
  • 以白教授 - Means 'reported to the teacher.'
  • 教授 - Refers to a teacher or instructor.
  • 责之曰 - Means 'scolded him and said.'
  • 士人而为异类所凭 - '士人' refers to a scholar or gentleman, '异类' means 'other beings' (ghosts or spirits), and '凭' means 'possessed by,' so the phrase means 'a scholar being possessed by other beings.'
  • 何至此 - Means 'how could this happen.'
  • 扣其所有 - Means 'asked about what he had.'
  • 袒服 - Refers to a loose-fitting garment.
  • 秽而无缝 - Means 'dirty and without seams.'
  • 命投诸火 - Means 'ordered to throw it into the fire.'
  • 踪迹 - Means 'trace' or 'follow the trail.'
  • 老圃 - Refers to an old gardener.
  • 向者小儿牧羊 - '向者' means 'previously,' '小儿' means 'little boy,' and '牧羊' means 'sheep herding,' so the phrase means 'previously a little boy was herding sheep.'
  • 牝羊坠西廓井中 - '牝羊' means 'female sheep,' '坠' means 'fell,' '西廓' means 'western gate,' and '井中' means 'into the well,' so the phrase means 'a female sheep fell into the well at the western gate.'
  • 不可取 - Means 'could not be retrieved.'
  • 呼道士行法咒 - '呼道士' means 'called a Taoist priest,' and '行法咒' means 'recited spells,' so the phrase means 'called a Taoist priest to recite spells.'
  • 黑豆投于井 - Means 'threw black beans into the well.'
  • 怪乃绝不至 - '怪' means 'ghost' or 'strange occurrence,' and '绝不至' means 'no longer came,' so the phrase means 'the ghost no longer came.'
  • 然董亦死 - Means 'but Dong also died.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • The Xuanhe era name of Emperor Huizong of Song (1119-1125).
  • A local government-sponsored educational institution during the Song dynasty.
  • A common literary device in Chinese ghost stories, often representing a spirit or ghost.
  • Refers to educated individuals, typically scholars or officials.
  • A teacher or instructor in the context of the prefectural school.

Proper Nouns

  • 董秀才 - Refers to Dong, a scholar or gentleman.
  • 教授 - Refers to the teacher or instructor at the prefectural school.
  • 西廓 - Refers to the western gate of the city.
  • 老圃 - Refers to an old gardener.

[3]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 已寝 - 已 means 'already,' and 寝 means 'sleep.' Together, it refers to the act of going to sleep.
  • 突而入 - 突 means 'suddenly' or 'burst,' and 入 means 'enter.' This phrase describes a sudden and forceful entry.
  • 隐不见 - 隐 means 'disappear' or 'vanish,' and 不见 means 'not visible.' This phrase describes the act of vanishing from sight.
  • 盖疫鬼云 - 盖 is a particle used to introduce a conclusion or explanation, 疫鬼 refers to 'epidemic spirits' or 'plague spirits,' and 云 is a particle used to introduce a statement or explanation. This phrase concludes that the event was caused by epidemic spirits.
  • 左右前后皆墓域 - 左右前后 means 'all around,' and 皆墓域 means 'all are burial grounds.' This phrase describes the surrounding area as being filled with burial sites.
  • 丛柩相望 - 丛 means 'crowded,' 柩 means 'coffins,' and 相望 means 'in sight of each other.' This phrase describes coffins being so numerous that they can be seen from one to another.
  • 辄闻啾啾之声 - 辄 means 'always' or 'immediately,' 闻 means 'hear,' and 啾啾之声 means 'chirping sounds.' This phrase describes the constant hearing of chirping sounds.
  • 盖鬼区也 - 盖 is a particle used to introduce a conclusion or explanation, 鬼区 means 'ghostly area,' and 也 is a particle used to conclude a statement. This phrase concludes that the area is a ghostly place.
  • 丧子 - 丧 means 'lose' or 'die,' and 子 means 'son.' This phrase refers to the death of a son.
  • 入寺设水陆 - 入寺 means 'enter the temple,' 设 means 'arrange' or 'set up,' and 水陆 refers to a 'water-land' ritual, a Buddhist ceremony for the dead. This phrase describes entering the temple to arrange a water-land ritual.
  • 夜未半 - 夜 means 'night,' and 未半 means 'not yet halfway through.' This phrase refers to the early part of the night.
  • 阖寺闻山下戏笑 - 阖寺 means 'the entire temple,' 闻 means 'hear,' 山下 means 'at the foot of the mountain,' and 戏笑 means 'laughing and joking.' This phrase describes the entire temple hearing laughing and joking at the foot of the mountain.
  • 往来交相问劳 - 往来 means 'coming and going,' 交相 means 'interacting with each other,' and 问劳 means 'asking after each other's welfare.' This phrase describes people interacting and asking after each other's welfare.
  • 自支径黄泥路口归 - 自 means 'from,' 支径 means 'side path,' 黄泥路口 means 'a fork in the road near the yellow mud,' and 归 means 'return.' This phrase describes returning from a side path near a fork in the road.
  • 逢二人同行 - 逢 means 'encounter,' 二人 means 'two people,' and 同行 means 'walking together.' This phrase describes encountering two people walking together.
  • 厉声曰 - 厉声 means 'in a stern voice,' and 曰 means 'said.' This phrase describes speaking in a stern voice.
  • 吾辈以寺中会集 - 吾辈 means 'we,' 以 means 'because,' 寺中 means 'in the temple,' and 会集 means 'gather.' This phrase describes gathering in the temple.
  • 见召而往 - 见召 means 'being summoned,' and 而往 means 'and went.' This phrase describes being summoned and going.
  • 汝何为者 - 汝 means 'you,' 何为 means 'what are you doing,' and 者 is a particle used to introduce a statement. This phrase asks what the person is doing.
  • 追逐欲殴之 - 追逐 means 'chase,' 欲 means 'want to,' and 殴之 means 'hit him/her.' This phrase describes chasing and wanting to hit someone.
  • 奔窜 - 奔 means 'run,' and 窜 means 'flee.' This phrase describes running and fleeing.
  • 适有篝火从寺出者 - 适 means 'just then,' 有 means 'there was,' 篝火 means 'campfire,' 从 means 'from,' 寺 means 'temple,' and 出者 means 'coming out.' This phrase describes a campfire coming out from the temple just then.
  • 乃得脱 - 乃 means 'then,' and 得脱 means 'managed to escape.' This phrase describes managing to escape.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 绍兴六年 - 绍兴 is the era name of Emperor Gaozong of Song, and 六年 means 'sixth year.' This refers to the year 1136 AD.
  • 余干 - A county in Jiangxi Province, China.
  • 疫鬼 - Refers to spirits believed to cause epidemics or plagues.
  • 芝山 - A mountain located in the city of Jiujiang, Jiangxi Province.
  • 城北一里 - 城北 means 'north of the city,' and 一里 means 'one li' (an ancient Chinese unit of distance, approximately 500 meters). This describes the location of the mountain.
  • 僧寺 - Refers to a Buddhist temple.
  • 两庑 - 两 means 'two,' and 庑 means 'side rooms' or 'pavilions.' This refers to the side rooms of the temple.
  • 丛柩相望 - Describes a scene where coffins are so numerous that they can be seen from one to another, indicating a crowded burial ground.
  • 绍兴十六年 - 绍兴 is the era name of Emperor Gaozong of Song, and 十六年 means 'sixteenth year.' This refers to the year 1146 AD.
  • 通判 - An official position in the Song Dynasty, responsible for overseeing local administration and assisting the governor.
  • 任良臣伯颜 - The name of the official who lost his son.
  • 水陆 - Refers to a 'water-land' ritual, a Buddhist ceremony for the dead.
  • 程祠部 - Refers to an official in the Song Dynasty, likely a high-ranking official.
  • 守墓仆 - A servant responsible for guarding a tomb.
  • 支径黄泥路口 - A side path near a fork in the road, possibly referring to a specific location.
  • 厉声曰 - Describes speaking in a stern voice, often used to convey authority or anger.
  • 吾辈以寺中会集 - Describes a group of people gathering in the temple for a specific purpose.
  • 见召而往 - Describes being summoned and going to a place.
  • 汝何为者 - Asks what the person is doing, often used in a confrontational or questioning tone.
  • 追逐欲殴之 - Describes chasing and wanting to hit someone, indicating aggression.
  • 奔窜 - Describes running and fleeing, often used in a desperate or panicked context.
  • 适有篝火从寺出者 - Describes a campfire coming out from the temple, possibly indicating a disturbance or activity.

[4]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 去城二十里 - Literally means 'twenty miles away from the city,' indicating a distance from the city.
  • 大姓饶氏居之 - Means 'the wealthy family of Rao resides there,' where '大姓' refers to a prominent or wealthy family.
  • 家人尝出游林间 - Means 'family members once went out for a walk in the forest,' with '尝' indicating a past occurrence.
  • 见仆柳，中空，函水可鉴 - Describes a willow tree that is hollow inside and can hold water, which can be used as a mirror. '函水可鉴' is a metaphorical expression suggesting clarity or reflection.
  • 子妇戏窥之，应时得疾 - Means 'the daughter-in-law playfully peered into it and immediately fell ill,' with '应时' indicating an immediate reaction.
  • 归家即痴卧，不复知之 - Means 'she returned home and lay in a daze, no longer aware of anything,' with '痴卧' suggesting a state of confusion or stupor.
  • 遂有物语于空中，与人酬酢往来 - Means 'then there was a voice speaking from the air, interacting with people,' with '酬酢' referring to reciprocal conversation or exchange.
  • 闻人歌声，辄能和，宛转如扬，韵有余 - Means 'it could hear human singing and respond in harmony, turning gracefully like a rising melody, with lingering resonance,' with '宛转如扬' describing a smooth and melodious sound.
  • 音律小误，必嗤笑指摘 - Means 'if there was a slight error in the melody, it would laugh and point out the mistake,' with '嗤笑' indicating mockery or ridicule.
  • 论文谈诗，率亦中理 - Means 'it could discuss literature and poetry, generally making sense,' with '率亦中理' suggesting a reasonable or logical approach.
  • 相去咫尺而莫见其形 - Means 'it was so close yet invisible in form,' with '咫尺' indicating a very short distance.
  • 仆妾有过，则对主人显言 - Means 'if servants or concubines did something wrong, it would speak openly to the master,' with '显言' indicating direct or open communication.
  • 虽数十里外田畴出纳为欺，亦即日举白 - Means 'even if something was done hundreds of miles away, it would immediately reveal it,' with '举白' indicating the act of revealing or exposing.
  • 无一讳隐 - Means 'nothing was hidden or concealed,' with '讳隐' indicating secrecy or concealment.
  • 上下积忿厌苦，殴攘祷禬，百术备至，终无所益 - Means 'the family accumulated anger and frustration, resorting to violence, prayer, and various other methods, but ultimately achieving nothing,' with '百术备至' indicating the use of every possible method.
  • 饶氏焚香拜祷曰 - Means 'the Rao family burned incense and prayed, saying,' with '焚香拜祷' indicating a ritual of worship or supplication.
  • 荷尊神惠顾，为日久 - Means 'we have been favored by your divine presence for a long time,' with '荷尊神惠顾' expressing gratitude for divine favor.
  • 人神异路，惧不敢媟慢，以为神羞 - Means 'humans and spirits have different paths, and we fear to be disrespectful, which would shame the spirit,' with '媟慢' indicating disrespect or rudeness.
  • 欲立新庙于山间，香火像设，丰洁祗事，愿神徒居之，各安其分，不亦善乎 - Means 'we wish to build a new temple in the mountains, with incense, altars, and offerings, and hope that the spirit will move to the new temple, so that both parties can be content,' with '徒居之' indicating relocation.
  • 许诺，自是寂无影响 - Means 'the spirit agreed, and from then on, there was no further contact,' with '寂无影响' indicating silence or absence of influence.
  • 饶氏自喜得计，营一庙甚华 - Means 'the Rao family was pleased with their plan and built a very grand temple,' with '营一庙甚华' indicating the construction of a magnificent temple.
  • 卜日迎以祠 - Means 'they chose a day to invite the spirit to the temple,' with '卜日' indicating divination to choose a favorable day.
  • 越五日至，言谑如初 - Means 'five days later, it arrived, and its playful remarks were as before,' with '言谑如初' indicating the continuation of playful behavior.
  • 饶翁责之曰 - Means 'the elder of the Rao family scolded it, saying,' with '饶翁' referring to the head of the Rao family.
  • 既庙食矣，又为吾祟何也 - Means 'you are now being worshipped in the temple, why do you still torment us?' with '庙食' indicating being worshipped in a temple.
  • 笑曰 - Means 'it laughed, saying,' with '笑曰' indicating a response in laughter.
  • 吾岂痴儿耶！如此高堂大屋，舍之而去，乃顾一小庙哉 - Means 'am I a fool? With such a grand hall and large house, why would I leave and go to a small temple?' with '高堂大屋' indicating a grand building.
  • 饶氏愈益沮丧 - Means 'the Rao family became increasingly disheartened,' with '愈益沮丧' indicating growing despair.
  • 讫子妇死，鬼始谢去，一家为之衰替云 - Means 'until the daughter-in-law died, the spirit finally left, and the family declined as a result,' with '衰替' indicating decline or deterioration.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 抚州述陂 - Refers to a specific location, possibly a village or area in the city of Fuzhou.
  • 大姓饶氏 - Refers to the prominent Rao family, a common surname in China.
  • 甘林 - A place of natural beauty, possibly a forest or grove.
  • 子妇 - Refers to the daughter-in-law of the family.
  • 仆柳 - Refers to a willow tree, which is often associated with grace and beauty in Chinese culture.
  • 庙食 - The practice of worshipping spirits in temples, a common religious practice in traditional Chinese society.
  • 卜日 - The practice of divination to choose a favorable day for important events, such as temple construction or rituals.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • 家人尝出游林间，见仆柳，中空，函水可鉴 - This sentence describes a sequence of events: the family went out for a walk, saw a willow tree, and noticed its hollow interior that could hold water. The sentence is clear but requires careful translation to maintain the flow.
  • 子妇戏窥之，应时得疾，归家即痴卧，不复知之 - This sentence describes the daughter-in-law's reaction to the willow tree. The sequence of events is clear, but the cause-and-effect relationship needs to be preserved.
  • 遂有物语于空中，与人酬酢往来 - This sentence introduces the supernatural element of a voice speaking from the air. The interaction with people needs to be clearly conveyed.
  • 闻人歌声，辄能和，宛转如扬，韵有余 - This sentence describes the voice's ability to mimic and harmonize with human singing. The musical quality needs to be preserved.
  • 音律小误，必嗤笑指摘 - This sentence describes the voice's critical nature. The tone of mockery needs to be conveyed.
  • 论文谈诗，率亦中理 - This sentence describes the voice's ability to discuss literature and poetry. The logical and reasonable nature of the discussion needs to be preserved.
  • 相去咫尺而莫见其形 - This sentence describes the proximity of the voice to the people but its invisibility. The contrast between closeness and invisibility needs to be maintained.
  • 仆妾有过，则对主人显言 - This sentence describes the voice's role in reporting the actions of servants and concubines. The direct and open communication needs to be conveyed.
  • 虽数十里外田畴出纳为欺，亦即日举白 - This sentence describes the voice's omniscience and ability to reveal hidden actions. The immediacy and thoroughness of the revelation need to be preserved.
  • 无一讳隐 - This sentence emphasizes the voice's lack of secrecy. The transparency needs to be conveyed.
  • 上下积忿厌苦，殴攘祷禬，百术备至，终无所益 - This sentence describes the family's frustration and the various methods they tried to deal with the voice. The futility of their efforts needs to be preserved.
  • 饶氏焚香拜祷曰 - This sentence describes the Rao family's ritual of worship and supplication. The sincerity and desperation of their prayers need to be conveyed.

[5]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 武昌李主簿 - Refers to a minor official from Wuchang, a city in Hubei Province.
  • 夢就逮冥司 - Literal expression meaning 'dreamed of being summoned to the underworld.'
  • 主者問汝前生為張氏子時 - 'The master asked you, when you were the son of the Zhang family in your previous life.'
  • 安得推妻墮水 - 'How could you push your wife into the water?' Question posed to test the subject's memory or honesty.
  • 妻自失足墮水死 - 'Your wife fell into the water and died accidentally,' clarifying the true cause of death.
  • 主者追本處山川之神供證 - 'The master sought evidence from the local mountain and river deities.'
  • 與李言同 - 'Which matched what Li said,' indicating consistency in the testimony.
  • 遂放還 - 'He was then released,' meaning the subject was allowed to return to life.
  • 他日，在旅舍遇婦人 - 'Some days later, he met a woman at an inn,' describing a subsequent encounter.
  • 自言為前生妻 - 'She claimed to be your wife from a previous life,' suggesting a reincarnation theme.
  • 相守不肯舍 - 'She clung to him, unwilling to let go,' indicating a strong emotional bond.
  • 绸缪如生 - 'They were intimate as if they were alive,' describing a close relationship.
  • 姻党皆知之 - 'All relatives and friends knew about it,' emphasizing the public nature of the relationship.
  • 數年乃謝去 - 'After several years, she finally left,' indicating a prolonged period of cohabitation.
  • 李亦不娶終身 - 'Li did not marry again for the rest of his life,' showing his commitment to the woman.
  • 雖無他苦，但常病腰痛 - 'Although he had no other suffering, he often suffered from lower back pain,' describing a persistent physical ailment.
  • 以木為兩椎，刳其中，每人日扣擊數十下，痛則少解 - 'He made two wooden clubs, hollowed out the inside, and struck himself dozens of times each day, which alleviated the pain slightly,' describing a self-treatment method.
  • 蓋鬼氣染渍所致云 - 'This was likely caused by the lingering influence of the ghostly atmosphere,' providing a supernatural explanation for the pain.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 武昌 - A city in Hubei Province, historically significant for its role in various dynasties.
  • 冥司 - The underworld or the realm of the dead, a common theme in Chinese folklore and literature.
  • 前生 - Previous life, a concept rooted in Buddhist and Taoist beliefs in reincarnation.
  • 張氏子 - The son of the Zhang family, indicating a specific lineage.
  • 山川之神 - Local deities associated with mountains and rivers, important in traditional Chinese religion.
  • 姻党 - Relatives and friends, emphasizing the social network around the main characters.

Proper Nouns

  • 武昌 - A city in Hubei Province.
  • 李主簿 - A minor official from Wuchang.
  • 張氏子 - The son of the Zhang family.
  • 山川之神 - Local deities associated with mountains and rivers.

[6]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 洪州州学正 - Refers to the headmaster or principal of the prefectural school in Hongzhou (modern-day Nanchang, Jiangxi Province).
  • 天性刻薄 - Describes a person with an inherently harsh or stingy nature.
  • 老而益甚 - Means 'even more so as one grows older,' indicating that the person's harshness increases with age.
  • 虽生徒告假，亦靳固不与 - '靳固' means to be stingy or reluctant, and '不与' means 'not to give.' This phrase indicates that even when students request leave, the person is unwilling to grant it.
  • 学官给五日 - Refers to the official granting of five days of leave, which the person reduces.
  • 他皆称是 - Means 'all the rest are the same,' indicating a consistent pattern of behavior.
  • 众憾之 - Means 'the people resented him,' expressing the collective dissatisfaction.
  • 张鬼子 - A nickname given to someone because his appearance resembled a ghost.
  • 以形容似鬼得名 - Literally means 'named because his appearance resembled a ghost,' indicating that he was called 'Ghost Zhang' due to his looks.
  • 弄假须似真 - Means 'to make the false seem real,' emphasizing the importance of convincing performance.
  • 要得一冥司牒乃可 - '冥司牒' refers to a document from the underworld, and '乃可' means 'only then can it be done.' This phrase indicates that a document from the underworld is required for the performance.
  • 牒式当如何 - Asks about the format of the document.
  • 曾见人为之 - Means 'I have seen it done before,' indicating that the speaker has prior experience with such documents.
  • 以白矾细书 - '白矾' is a type of white alum, and '细书' means 'fine writing.' This phrase describes the method of writing the document.
  • 自押字于后 - Means 'signed it at the end,' indicating that the person signed the document.
  • 是夜诣州学 - Means 'that night he went to the prefectural school,' describing the timing and location of the event.
  • 学门已扃 - '扃' means 'locked,' indicating that the school gate was closed.
  • 鬼子入于隙间 - Means 'Ghost Zhang entered through a crack,' describing how he entered the school.
  • 张老见之，怒曰 - '张老' refers to the old headmaster, and '怒曰' means 'angrily said,' indicating the headmaster's reaction.
  • 畜生，何敢然 - A derogatory term for animals, used to express anger and disbelief.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 至元甲午秋丁 - Classical date format; '至元' is the reign era of Kublai Khan (Yuan dynasty), '甲午' is the year in the Chinese sexagenary cycle, and '秋丁' refers to a specific day in the autumn season.
  • 诣吉水文庙陪拜 - '诣' means to go to; '文庙' is the Confucian temple; '陪拜' means to accompany in worship or to participate in a ritual.
  • 户扃鐍如故 - '户' is the door; '扃' is a door bolt; '鐍' is a lock. The phrase means the door was locked as before, indicating that no one had entered or exited.
  • 主人大骇，欲闻官 - The host was greatly frightened and wanted to report to the authorities.
  • 中夜恍惚间 - A time in the middle of the night, in a state of confusion or dreamlike state.
  • 三人挽之同归 - '挽' means to pull or drag; '同归' means to return together. This suggests that the three figures dragged the person back with them.
  • 碎其人出门缝 - '碎其人' may be a metaphor or a miswriting of '碎其人出门缝' (literally 'to break open the door's seams'). It may mean that the door was forcibly opened or that the person was dragged through the door.
  • 特来拜庙 - '特来' means especially or specifically; '拜庙' is to worship at the temple. This indicates that the person had a specific purpose in visiting the temple.
  • 畏水不敢渡 - '畏水' is to fear water; '不敢渡' is to dare not cross. This suggests the person was afraid of crossing water.
  • 遂止不行 - '遂止' means he stopped; '不行' means he did not proceed. The person refused to move forward.
  • 拽之以渡 - '拽' is to drag or pull; '以渡' is to cross over. The three figures pulled the person across the water.
  • 诵玄帝咒 - '玄帝' is the Black Emperor, a deity in Daoist tradition; '咒' is a magical incantation or spell. This is a reference to Daoist ritual.
  • 拽之遍行洲上 - '拽之' is to drag the person; '遍行洲上' means to walk around the entire land or area. The person was dragged around the area.
  • 但及水则又极力挽回 - '但及水' means as soon as they reached the water; '极力挽回' means to try with great effort to pull back. This indicates the person resisted crossing the water.
  • 抚其面曰 - '抚其面' means to touch or pat someone’s face; '曰' is to say. This is a gesture of communication or reprimand.
  • 何误两字 - '何误' means why is there an error; '两字' means two characters. The figure is questioning why two characters were mispronounced or misread.
  • 未几，闻钟声 - '未几' means not long after; '闻钟声' is to hear the sound of a bell. This suggests the supernatural beings left after the bell rang.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The sentence '潭州有清净觉地，宋咸淳间，游士胡天俊寓焉' introduces the setting and the main character, Hu Tianjun, who stayed in the serene place.
  • The sentence '月照抚琴梅树下，遥见美女欲前且却' describes the scene where Hu plays the qin under the moonlight and sees a woman hesitating to approach.
  • The sequence '胡作意三弄，女迤逦近前，胡迎揖之' describes Hu playing three pieces with intent, the woman approaching, and Hu greeting her respectfully.
  • The woman's voice is described as harmonious and sorrowful, indicating her unfulfilled desires.
  • Hu expresses his belief that they are true kindred spirits, meeting by fate.
  • The woman leaves, promising to meet him on the next clear night.
  • Hu is distracted by his friends and forgets the appointment, leading to regret.
  • Hu finds a handkerchief with a poem, which he later shows to others.
  • The poem describes the natural setting and the woman's emotional state.
  • The woman stands gazing at the clouds, wiping away tears from the plum blossoms.
  • Hu is melancholic and goes to sleep.
  • Hu shows the handkerchief to Zhao Binghu, who is shocked.
  • Zhao Binghu reveals that the handkerchief belonged to his deceased wife, Qiao Shi.
  • Hu pours wine as an offering and composes a poem.
  • The poem expresses Hu's regret for missing the appointment.
  • The poem imagines the woman's spirit stepping on the waning moon and the natural imagery of dew-soaked clothes.

  • 商女不知亡国恨，隔江犹唱后庭花 - Another line from Du Mu's poem, criticizing the courtesans for not understanding the sorrow of a fallen nation while singing the same song.
  • 长嗟入林间不见 - '长嗟' means 'to sigh deeply,' and '入林间不见' means 'disappeared into the forest.'
  • 明夕夫归，言之，伏入江浒 - '明夕' means 'the next day,' '夫归' means 'the husband returned,' '言之' means 'told him,' and '伏入江浒' means 'hid by the riverside.'
  • 翌日，视乃一新坟，询知侯将士葬妻裴氏也 - '翌日' means 'the next day,' '视乃一新坟' means 'saw a new grave,' '询知' means 'inquired and found out,' '侯将士' means 'a general's soldiers,' and '葬妻裴氏也' means 'buried his wife, Lady Pei.'
  • 侯置一妾，推溺之，自戕于林间，就葬焉 - '侯置一妾' means 'the general had a concubine,' '推溺之' means 'threw her into the water,' '自戕于林间' means 'killed himself in the forest,' and '就葬焉' means 'was buried there.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The sentence '其暮擢谢舟人而去，与沈坐汴堤月中' introduces Zhe Zhe and his interaction with Shen, setting the scene for the conversation that follows.
  • This question '兄识擢何人也' asks Shen if he knows who Zhe Zhe is, establishing the context for the subsequent revelation.
  • This statement '非也，擢乃冥官' reveals Zhe Zhe's true identity as a spirit official, adding a supernatural element to the narrative.
  • This explanation '阴道公事，故不任昼，乃得与兄同游' clarifies why Zhe Zhe can only handle public affairs at night and why he can now interact with Shen.
  • This statement '今去阴迁阳，托孕于亲已五载矣' explains Zhe Zhe's current situation, having been pregnant in his mother's womb for five years.
  • This phrase '步步邀兄者，意有所托' indicates that Zhe Zhe has been inviting Shen gradually, with a specific purpose in mind.
  • This request '祈兄与醒之耳' asks Shen to help Zhe Zhe awaken or enlighten.
  • This statement '寓亲腹中，其家以为不祥' describes Zhe Zhe's condition and the family's perception of it as an omen.
  • This clause '竭货而为，擢尚未往，神固何为' explains the family's efforts and the lack of divine intervention.
  • This instruction '兄可往其家，朱书产字，令吞之，擢即生矣' tells Shen what to do to help Zhe Zhe be born.

  • 敢拒神明 - 'Dare to defy the divine,' a hyperbolic statement used to emphasize the speaker’s helplessness.
  • 容至天明 - 'Allow until dawn,' a request for time.

  • 杖之数十 - 'Beat him dozens of times,' a punishment.
  • 产蓐虚嬴 - 'Weakened from childbirth,' a medical term referring to postpartum weakness.
  • 正气不足 - 'Deficient in righteous qi (vital energy),' a traditional Chinese medical concept.

  • 可决一百，配流东海之南 - Literal meaning: 'can be sentenced to one hundred strokes, exiled to the south of the East Sea.' '决' means 'sentence,' '配流' means 'exile,' and '东海之南' means 'south of the East Sea.'

  • 齐氏禄命实有二十八年 - Literal meaning: 'Qi's fortune and fate actually have twenty-eight years.' '禄命' means 'fortune and fate,' and '实有' means 'actually have.'
  • 诚愿却回 - Literal meaning: 'truly wishes to return.' '诚愿' means 'truly wishes,' and '却回' means 'return.'
  • 付案勒回 - Literal meaning: 'hand it over to the case and order a return.' '付案' means 'hand it over to the case,' and '勒回' means 'order a return.'
  • 事事皆隳 - Literal meaning: 'everything is destroyed.' '事事' means 'everything,' and '皆隳' means 'all destroyed.'
  • 魂与生人，事有何异 - Literal meaning: 'a soul and a living person, what is the difference.' '魂' means 'soul,' '生人' means 'living person,' and '事有何异' means 'what is the difference.'
  • 年满当死之日，病笃而无尸耳 - Literal meaning: 'on the day when the year is up and death is due, the person is gravely ill but has no body.' '年满当死之日' means 'on the day when the year is up and death is due,' '病笃' means 'gravely ill,' and '无尸耳' means 'has no body.'
  • 生魂只有此异 - Literal meaning: 'a living soul only differs in this way.' '生魂' means 'living soul,' and '只有此异' means 'only differs in this way.'
  • 拜请之 - Literal meaning: 'bowed and requested.' '拜请' means 'bowed and requested,' and '之' is a particle indicating the object of the action.
  • 遂令齐氏同归 - Literal meaning: 'then ordered Qi to return together.' '遂令' means 'then ordered,' '齐氏' means 'Qi,' and '同归' means 'return together.'
  • 各拜而出 - Literal meaning: 'each bowed and left.' '各拜' means 'each bowed,' and '而出' means 'and left.'
  • 黄衫人复引南行 - Literal meaning: 'the man in yellow robes led south again.' '黄衫人' means 'man in yellow robes,' and '复引南行' means 'led south again.'
  • 即出其城 - Literal meaning: 'immediately left the city.' '即' means 'immediately,' '出' means 'left,' and '其城' means 'the city.'
  • 若行崖谷 - Literal meaning: 'as if walking through cliffs and valleys.' '若' means 'as if,' '行' means 'walking,' and '崖谷' means 'cliffs and valleys.'
  • 足跌而堕 - Literal meaning: 'fell down with a stumble.' '足跌' means 'fell down,' and '而堕' means 'with a stumble.'
  • 开目，即复跪在案前 - Literal meaning: 'opened his/her eyes, and immediately knelt in front of the desk.' '开目' means 'opened his/her eyes,' '即' means 'immediately,' '复跪' means 'knelt again,' and '在案前' means 'in front of the desk.'

  • 黄衫人 - A man in yellow robes, possibly a guide or messenger in the afterlife.

  • 使君始闻韦之将到也 - The magistrate first heard that Wei was coming, indicating that the magistrate was informed of Wei's arrival.
  • 设馆施繐帐以待之 - Set up a hall and hang white curtains to await him, indicating preparation for Wei's arrival.
  • 及得书，惊骇，殊不信 - Upon receiving the letter, he was shocked and did not believe it, indicating disbelief and shock.

  • 而命其妻子以肩舆迓焉 - And ordered his wife and children to meet him with a sedan chair, indicating a formal reception.

  • 齐氏饮食生育无异于常 - Qi's diet, birth, and reproduction were no different from usual, indicating that Qi's daily life continued normally.
  • 但肩舆之 - But she was carried in a sedan chair, indicating that Qi was transported in a special manner.

  • 俄而犬顧泰曰 - The sudden appearance of the dog and its immediate speech might be interpreted as a supernatural event.
  • 乃许焉 - The agreement is clear, but the exact nature of the agreement (whether it was verbal or implied) might be ambiguous.
  • 犬忽化为人 - The transformation is a fantastical element, which might be interpreted differently in different contexts.

  • 行十余里，天曙 - '行十余里' means 'walked for more than ten miles,' and '天曙' means 'the sky grew light.' This indicates the passage of time and the change in the environment.
  • 逢耕人，问之曰 - '逢耕人' means 'met a farmer,' and '问之曰' means 'asked him, saying.' This indicates a conversation with a farmer.
  • 近有何墓 - '近' means 'near,' and '有何墓' means 'what tomb is there?' This is a question about the location of a tomb.
  • 对曰 - '对曰' means 'replied, saying.' This indicates the farmer's response.
  • 此十里内有晋朝并州刺史刘琨姬张宠奴墓 - '此十里内' means 'within ten miles,' '晋朝' means 'Jin Dynasty,' '并州刺史' means 'Governor of Bingzhou,' '刘琨' is a historical figure, '姬' means 'mistress,' and '张宠奴墓' means 'the tomb of Zhang Chongnu.' This provides a specific historical reference.
  • 乃知是昨夜所止也 - '乃知' means 'then knew,' '是' means 'that,' '昨夜' means 'last night,' and '所止' means 'where we stayed.' This reveals the realization of the protagonist.
  • 又三数里，路隅有朽髑髅 - '又三数里' means 'another few miles,' '路隅' means 'at the side of the road,' and '朽髑髅' means 'rotten skull.' This describes a macabre scene.
  • 旁有穿穴，草生其中 - '旁有穿穴' means 'there was a hole nearby,' and '草生其中' means 'grass grew inside it.' This provides additional details about the scene.
  • 泛视之若四眼 - '泛视之' means 'looked at it generally,' and '若四眼' means 'like it had four eyes.' This describes an unusual appearance.
  • 盖所谓召花眼也 - '盖' means 'it is said,' '所谓' means 'called,' and '召花眼' means 'the eye that summons flowers.' This is a reference to a specific type of eye.
  • 而思大头多眼者，杳不可知也 - '而思' means 'and think,' '大头多眼者' means 'a person with a large head and many eyes,' and '杳不可知也' means 'is completely unknown.' This expresses uncertainty about a specific figure.
  • 吾尝以儒视世界 - '吾尝' means 'I have often,' '以儒视世界' means 'viewed the world through the lens of Confucianism.' This indicates the protagonist's philosophical perspective.
  • 人死固有鬼 - '人死' means 'when people die,' '固' means 'certainly,' and '有鬼' means 'there are ghosts.' This expresses a belief in the existence of ghosts.
  • 以释观之，轮回之义理亦昭然 - '以释观之' means 'from the perspective of Buddhism,' '轮回' means 'reincarnation,' and '义理亦昭然' means 'the principles are also clear.' This indicates a belief in reincarnation.
  • 奈何此妓牢落千载，犹歌于冥冥之中 - '奈何' means 'how can it be that,' '此妓' means 'this courtesan,' '牢落千载' means 'has been forgotten for a thousand years,' and '犹歌于冥冥之中' means 'still sings in the depths of the underworld.' This expresses surprise and wonder.
  • 则信乎视听之表，圣贤亦有不言者也 - '则信乎' means 'then it is true that,' '视听之表' means 'beyond what can be seen or heard,' '圣贤' means 'sages and virtuous people,' and '亦有不言者也' means 'there are things they do not speak of.' This expresses a philosophical reflection on the limits of human knowledge.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 即登科 - Indicates immediate success in the imperial examination, a significant achievement.
  • 调台州教授 - Refers to being appointed as a teacher in Taizhou, a position of respect and influence.
  • 将往建康谒府尹 - Means planning to go to Jiankang to pay respects to the governor, a common practice among officials and scholars.
  • 常怀绝命之意 - Indicates the concubine often harbors thoughts of suicide, reflecting deep emotional distress.
  • 吾去后，切勿加以楚虐 - Means 'After I leave, please do not subject her to cruel treatment,' reflecting the hierarchical nature of Chinese family relationships.
  • 万一有不虞，恐费经护 - Means 'If something unexpected happens, it might be too late to take care of her,' highlighting practical considerations in managing household affairs.
  • 必不可畜，侯归日去之，不难也 - Means 'If it becomes absolutely necessary, I will send her away when I return, which won't be difficult,' showing a pragmatic approach to family management.
  • 时方侨寓他处（不记其地） - Means 'At that time, they were temporarily living elsewhere (the location is not remembered),' suggesting a transient lifestyle.
  • 数日到建康，已解担，闻耳畔啾啾人声，似其妾 - Means 'After several days, he arrived in Jiankang, having unloaded his baggage, and heard a whispering voice near his ear, which seemed to be that of his concubine,' creating a sense of unease and mystery.
  • 而不见形状，问之，泣曰 - Means 'But he could not see her shape. When he asked, she wept and said,' adding to the eerie atmosphere.
  • 君才出门，既遭棰，势不复可生，自经死矣 - Means 'As soon as you stepped out, I was beaten, and I could no longer live, so I hanged myself,' suggesting a tragic and violent end.
  • 季为之哀泣解谢，欲回车 - Means 'Jì mourned and apologized, wanting to turn back,' showing his deep emotional response and desire to protect his concubine.
  • 念业已至，欲弗信，又不忍，姑遣仆兼程归扣其事 - Means 'But thinking that he had already arrived, he wanted to disbelieve it but couldn't bear to, so he sent a servant back home to check the matter,' reflecting the practical steps taken to address the issue.
  • 且为家人作牒 - Means 'Also had the servant write a document for the family,' showing the importance of formal communication and record-keeping.
  • 经邑，仍略疏葬埋之费 - Means 'Passing through the county, the servant still briefly mentioned the cost of burial,' indicating financial and logistical considerations.
  • 自是继夕哀泣 - Means 'From then on, he wept every night,' showing ongoing grief and concern.
  • 及仆还，云宅内固全无事，某到时，侍人自持饭饭我 - Means 'When the servant returned, he said that there was indeed nothing wrong at home, and when he arrived, the servant brought food for him,' suggesting that the initial fear was unfounded.
  • 是晚复至，季正色责之曰 - Means 'That evening, she came again, and Jì sternly rebuked her, saying,' demonstrating his authority and determination.
  • 汝为何等妖厉，顾敢然不亟去，吾将请集道流，绳汝以法！ - Means 'What kind of evil spirit are you? How dare you not leave immediately? I will summon Taoist priests to punish you according to the law!,' reflecting the belief in supernatural forces and the power of religious authority.
  • 今但从君丐佛经数卷，薄奠楮钱而已 - Means 'Now I just ask for a few scrolls of Buddhist scriptures and a small offering of paper money,' showing a pragmatic approach to dealing with the supernatural.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 初南渡 - Initially crossed south, referring to the act of migrating south, likely during the Southern Song period following the fall of the Northern Song to the Jurchens.
  • 寓家湖州德清驿 - Stayed at the Deyang Post in Deqing, Huzhou. This is a formal way of saying the person temporarily resided at a government-run inn.
  • 湫隘不足容 - Too narrow and cramped to hold [a family]. *湫隘* means 'low and narrow,' often used to describe poor living conditions.
  • 谋居于临安甚切 - Strongly desired to settle in Lin’an. *谋居* means 'to plan to settle,' and *甚切* means 'very urgent' or 'very strongly.'
  • 才为钱三千缗 - Cost only three thousand strings of cash. *缗* is a unit of currency, typically 1,000 coins tied together in a string.
  • 都城中如此第舍，当直五万缗 - Such a mansion in the capital should be worth five thousand strings of cash. *第舍* refers to a residence or mansion.
  • 今不能什一，亦知其说乎？ - Now it costs only one-tenth of that, do you understand the reason? *什一* is a classical way of saying 'one-tenth.'
  • 是宅久为妖厉所据，人不复堪处 - This house has long been occupied by malevolent spirits, and people can no longer endure living there. *妖厉* refers to supernatural or evil forces.
  • 故以相付耳 - Therefore, it is given to you. *相付* is a polite way of saying 'to give over' or 'to entrust.'
  • 邢之内子及姑庆国夫人者，皆倡演不可用 - Xing’s wife and his mother-in-law, Lady Qingguo, both strongly opposed the use of the house. *倡演* means 'to advocate or argue against.'
  • 邢患之，稍稍语内子曰 - Xing was troubled by this and gradually told his wife. *患之* means 'was troubled by it.'
  • 人言是否，固未定，我将先验之 - Whether what people say is true or not is still uncertain; I will go and check it myself. *验之* means 'to test or verify.'
  • 盍择谨厚二婢子偕行，庶可证其实 - Why not choose two diligent and honest maids to go with you, so that you may confirm the truth? *盍* is a rhetorical particle meaning 'why not?'
  • 凡留半月，中外奥僻，无不临履 - They stayed for half a month, and every part of the house, inside and out, was examined. *中外奥僻* means 'the inner and outer parts, even the hidden and secluded areas.'
  • 夜寝于正堂，寂无一怖 - They slept in the main hall at night, and there was not a single moment of fear. *寂无一怖* means 'completely silent, not a bit afraid.'
  • 归家具言，且力诋前妄 - Upon returning, they all reported the situation, and strongly refuted the previous claim as false. *诋* means 'to denounce or refute.'
  • 二婢亦深赞屋庐之美 - The two maids also greatly praised the beauty of the house. *深赞* means 'deeply praised.'
  • 始尽家徒之 - Thus, the whole family moved in. *徒之* is a classical way of saying 'to move to a place.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 邢太尉（孝扬） - General Xing (Xiaoyang) – a title and name. *太尉* is a high military or administrative title, often used as a honorific or posthumous title. *孝扬* is likely the name or courtesy name of the person.
  • 湖州德清驿 - The Deyang Post in Deqing, Huzhou – a government-run驿站 (yìzhàn), or post station, in the county of Deqing, Huzhou province. These were common along the Grand Canal and used by officials traveling between the north and south.
  • 临安 - Lin’an – the capital of the Southern Song dynasty, modern-day Hangzhou. It was the political and cultural center of southern China after the fall of the Northern Song.
  • 妖厉 - Evil spirits or malevolent forces. This reflects traditional Chinese beliefs in supernatural entities that could inhabit abandoned or cursed places.
  • 内子 - Wife – a term used by men to refer to their spouse, often used in classical and formal contexts.
  • 姑庆国夫人 - Lady Qingguo, the mother-in-law. *姑* is a term of respect for a mother-in-law, and *庆国夫人* is a title indicating a noble or high-ranking lady.
  • 三千缗 - Three thousand strings of cash. The *缗* was a unit of currency in ancient China, often used in official or large-scale transactions.
  • 五万缗 - Five thousand strings of cash. This shows the contrast between the cost of the house and the perceived value.
  • 尽家徒之 - Move the whole family. This reflects the social custom of relocating to a new residence, especially when moving to a new city or region.

Proper Nouns

  • 邢太尉（孝扬） - Xing Taiwei (Xiaoyang) – A title and name. *太尉* is a title, and *孝扬* is likely the name or courtesy name.
  • 湖州德清驿 - The Deyang Post in Deqing, Huzhou – A specific location in the Southern Song period.
  • 临安 - Lin’an – The capital of the Southern Song dynasty, modern-day Hangzhou.
  • 庆国夫人 - Lady Qingguo – A title for the mother-in-law, indicating nobility or high status.
  • 内子 - Wife – A term used by men to refer to their spouse.
  • 二婢子 - Two maids – Refers to female servants or attendants.
  • 正堂 - Main hall – The central room of a house, often used for important functions or gatherings.
  • 第舍 - Residence or mansion – A term for a house or estate.
